facts.text: Garden City was founded in 1869 by Alexander Turney Stewart, a department store magnate, as one of America's first planned communities.
Stewart also built the Central Railroad of Long Island, giving Garden City its own rail line that connected to Long Island City and Farmingdale.
The Cathedral of the Incarnation, a Gothic Revival landmark, was erected by Stewart's wife Cornelia in memory of her husband after his death in 1876.
The village hosts the Cradle of Aviation Museum, celebrating Long Island's deep aviation history, including early aircraft and the 1920s airfield.
Garden City’s historic Garden City Hotel once hosted Charles Lindbergh, who stayed there while preparing for his solo transatlantic flight.
The Waldorf School of Garden City, founded in 1947 as part of Adelphi University, was one of the first Waldorf schools in the United States.
